purpose: Predicts the order in which vol regime shifts propagate across asset classes to inform hedge timing and cross-asset monitoring.
components: Standard sequence: Rates vol first, then credit vol (1-3 week lag), then equity vol (2-6 week lag), then FX vol
Conditional reliability: ~70% for fundamentally-driven credit-origin transitions; ~40-50% unconditionally
Microstructure disruption: Positioning-driven transitions can skip states entirely (Feb 2018, March 2020)
Private credit opacity disruption: May cause rates to skip credit and propagate directly to FX then equity (no historical analogue)
Monitoring: CLO arbitrage collapse as credit-first signal; October 2023 confirms rates-first propagation
current_assessment: Unconditional 70% reliability was refuted — honest base rate closer to 40-50%. Conditional claim for fundamentally-driven credit-origin transitions survives at moderate confidence. Current monitoring should focus on CLO arbitrage dynamics and rates-to-credit transmission for the next transition. Microstructure-driven skip risk means sequential hedge timing may fail.
limitations: Unconditional base rate inflated by counting partial matches as matches
Timing varies significantly with shock magnitude
Private credit opacity may disrupt sequence in unprecedented ways
Microstructure-driven transitions bypass the framework entirely
Only 5 reference episodes with 2 clean breaks
